name: Visuals Toolkit V3

meta.description: Tables, charts, heatmaps, timelines, flowcharts, trees, dashboards, and metric grids. Dark-mode-safe HTML with Plotly, auto-resizing iframes, and fullscreen support. Pure ASCII/Markdown fallback when HTML is unavailable. Incompatible with Markdown Normalizer filter (disable it or turn off xml_tag_cleanup). required_open_webui_version: 0.4.0

manifest.title: Visuals Toolkit V3
manifest.author: Cole
manifest.description: Tables, charts, heatmaps, timelines, flowcharts, trees, dashboards, and metric grids. Dark-mode-safe HTML with Plotly, auto-resizing iframes, and fullscreen support. Pure ASCII/Markdown fallback when HTML is unavailable.

specs.name: render_chart
specs.description: Render a chart (ASCII in text mode, Plotly in embed mode).

specs.name: render_comparison_table
specs.description: Compare items across criteria with optional highlighting.

criteria.description: List of criteria to compare on

scores.description: Dict mapping item names to their criterion values

specs.name: render_dashboard
specs.description: Combine multiple visualizations into one dashboard.

specs.name: render_flowchart
specs.description:
Render an ASCII flowchart. Supports two formats:

1. List of strings: sequential box-drawing flowchart
["Start", "Process", "End"]

2. List of edge dicts: graph-style flowchart
[{"from": "A", "to": "B", "label": "yes"}]

steps.description: List of strings or edge dicts

specs.name: render_heatmap
specs.description: Render a heatmap (ASCII blocks in text mode, Plotly in embed).

data.description: 2D list of numeric values (rows x cols)

specs.name: render_metrics_grid
specs.description: Render key metrics in a card grid.

metrics.description: Dict of metric name to value

specs.name: render_multi_chart
specs.description: Render a chart with multiple series.

specs.name: render_table
specs.description: Render a table from a list of row objects.

rows.description: List of dict rows (keys become columns)

specs.name: render_tree
specs.description:
Render hierarchical data as a tree.

Accepts two formats:
1. Nested dict: {"src": {"main.py": None, "lib": {"utils.py": None}}}
2. Edge list: [{"parent": "src", "child": "main.py"}]

data.description: Nested dict or list of edge dicts
